edge_hypothesis: The MACD tail harvester champion (PF=1.712, DD=7.5%) uses 1.0x ATR stop — but the advisory's STOP_FLOOR directive mandates minimum 1.5x ATR. This creates a critical unresolved tension: is the 1.0x tight stop essential to MACD's edge, or does MACD work at wider stops too? ALL existing MACD Claude specs (mc7h1f6p, mc4h9x2t, mc9x2k7d) use 1.0x ATR stop. None have tested 1.5x. This spec tests MACD with doctrine-compliant 1.5x ATR stop and compensating higher R:R (7:1 through 10:1). THREE HYPOTHESES: (1) TIGHT-STOP-ESSENTIAL: If PF drops below 1.0 at 1.5x stop (vs PF=1.712 at 1.0x stop), the tight stop is critical — MACD's edge comes from cutting losers fast, and the wider stop lets losing trades accumulate damage. This would mean the STOP_FLOOR directive should be template-conditional, not universal. (2) STOP-AGNOSTIC: If PF remains above 1.3 at 1.5x stop with 10:1 R:R, MACD's signal quality is robust to stop width. This means the 1.0x stop was unnecessarily tight — fewer trades would hit the wider stop, potentially IMPROVING win rate. (3) STOP-WIDTH-OPTIMAL: If 1.5x stop + 10:1 R:R OUTPERFORMS 1.0x stop + 7:1 R:R, the champion's tight stop was actually HURTING performance by prematurely stopping out trades that would have recovered and hit the tail target. Win rate at 1.0x/7:1 was ~20% — if some of those stops were premature, 1.5x should improve win rate to ~22-25%. Combined with 10:1 R:R, even 1-2% win rate improvement would dramatically increase PF. Regime assumption: ALL (MACD 7:1 champion is all-regimes profitable). Invalidation: If PF < 0.9 at 1.5x stop across all variants, tight stops are the structural foundation of MACD tail harvesting — make the STOP_FLOOR directive conditional.

variants.name: macd_widestop_1h_10to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-line cross on ETH/1h with WIDE stop: 1.5x ATR stop / 15.0x ATR TP (10:1 R:R). The wide-stop flagship. Direct test of whether MACD's signal quality supports wider stops at extreme R:R. The 1.0x stop champion had ~20% win rate on 1h — meaning 80% of entries get stopped out. If a meaningful fraction of those stops were premature (price dipped below 1.0x ATR then recovered to eventually hit 7.0x+ ATR), the wider 1.5x stop would capture those recoveries. At 10:1 R:R, breakeven is 9.1% win rate. Even if wider stop only captures an additional 2-3% of trades as winners, PF improvement could be substantial. Expected: 170+ trades (same entry signal as champion), win rate 10-15% at 10:1 R:R.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0 to > 0 (histogram bullish zero-line cross).
Close > EMA_50 (trend confirmation — price above medium-term average).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0 to < 0 (histogram bearish zero-line cross).
Close < EMA_50 (trend confirmation — price below medium-term average).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend confirmation: Close > EMA_50 (long), Close < EMA_50 (short)
Wide stop test: 1.5x ATR (vs champion's 1.0x ATR) — STOP_FLOOR compliant
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (15.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
R:R = 10:1. Needs 9.1% win rate to break even after fees.
Wide-stop test: do premature 1.0x stops cost the champion wins that 1.5x would capture?
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 15.0x ATR_14 from entry.

ATR_14 for 1.5x stop (WIDE) / 15.0x TP — 10:1 R:R, STOP_FLOOR compliant

variants.name: macd_widestop_4h_9to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-line cross on ETH/4h with WIDE stop: 1.5x ATR stop / 13.5x ATR TP (9:1 R:R). The 4h MACD champion at 1.0x stop had PF=1.712 at 7:1 and PF=1.442 at 8:1 on 4h. The 4h R:R curve peaked at 7:1 then declined. With WIDER stop (1.5x), the curve may shift — wider stops could delay the decline point because the stop catches fewer premature exits on the higher timeframe where price swings are larger relative to ATR. 9:1 R:R at 1.5x stop means TP at 13.5x ATR — roughly equivalent effective distance to 1.0x stop / 9.0x TP but with more room for the trade to breathe. Breakeven: 10.0% win rate. Expected: 60-80 trades on 4h.

Take profit at 13.5x ATR_14 from entry.

variants.name: macd_widestop_1h_7to1_direct_compare
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-line cross on ETH/1h with 1.5x ATR stop / 10.5x ATR TP (7:1 R:R). DIRECT COMPARISON to the champion variant (1.0x stop / 7.0x TP, also 7:1 R:R). Same template, same asset, same timeframe, same R:R — only the stop width differs (1.0x vs 1.5x). This is the cleanest possible A/B test of stop width. The champion achieves PF=1.712 with ~161 trades and ~20% win rate at 1.0x/7.0x. This variant uses 1.5x/10.5x — same 7:1 ratio but wider absolute distances. If PF is HIGHER, wider stops rescue premature stopouts. If PF is LOWER, tight stops are better because they cut bad trades before additional damage accumulates. If PF is SIMILAR (~1.5-1.7), stop width doesn't matter much and the STOP_FLOOR can be applied to MACD without performance loss. Breakeven: 12.5% win rate. Expected: 161+ trades (identical entry signal).
